projects.name: Solar + Energy Storage on Garage
projects.description: Install 5-7 solar panels on south-facing garage roof face (20x12 ft, ~2-2.8 kW) plus battery storage. Solar = standalone ROI investment (~5yr payback at $0.42/kWh). Battery = generator alternative (no lot space for generator). 30% federal ITC applies to both when paired.
projects.notes: DESIGN: South garage face only. 5 portrait panels = 2.0 kW; 7 mixed = 2.8 kW. ~3,200-4,500 kWh/yr production (20-26% offset). All-in after ITC: solar ~$9-10k, battery ~$9-10k. VENDORS: Sunergy (sunergy.com) — EnergySage 2026 CA Installer of Year, Enphase Platinum, no door-to-door. NRG Clean Power (nrgcleanpower.com) — Diamond Certified, 35yr, San Mateo County, 40yr warranty. Tesla Energy (tesla.com/energy) — online only. AVOID: Sunrun.

tasks.title: Analyze energy usage and size system
tasks.notes: 17,178 kWh/yr at $0.42/kWh avg. Target: 5-7 panels south garage face (20x12 ft) = 2-2.8 kW. ~5yr payback after ITC.

tasks.title: Research and shortlist solar installers
tasks.notes: Shortlist: Sunergy (sunergy.com, Enphase Platinum, no door-to-door), NRG Clean Power (nrgcleanpower.com, Diamond Certified, San Mateo County), Tesla Energy (tesla.com/energy, online only). Avoid Sunrun.

tasks.title: Contact installers for quotes
tasks.notes: Spec: south garage face (20x12 ft, 5-7 panels, ~2-2.8 kW), paired battery (Enphase IQ or Powerwall 3), detached garage sub-panel interconnect. Ask each for: $/W, panel brand, inverter type, battery spec, warranty, timeline, ITC total. Request free shade/LIDAR analysis.

tasks.title: Compare quotes and select installer
tasks.notes: Prefer Enphase microinverters over string inverter. Compare $/W, battery spec, warranty, timeline. 30% ITC on full system incl. battery.

tasks.title: Confirm ITC timing — credit files in year PTO is granted
tasks.notes: 30% federal ITC on Form 5695. Claimed in tax year Permission to Operate granted by PG&E. Battery qualifies only when solar-paired. Plan install timing accordingly.

insights.summary: [entryway] An unknown person is moving inside the house.

Energy synthesis (May 31): 1. **Overnight Baseline:** The baseline load is approximately **2.87 kWh/hr**. This is likely driven by "always-on" essentials: the refrigerator, networking equipment (routers/modems), security systems, and standby power for electronics.

2. **Evening Spike:** The **21:00 PDT** spike, averaging **4.
Consistent evening energy spike: avg peak 4.71 kWh/hr typically around 21:00 PDT across 8 days. Likely AC/HVAC cooling cycle. At $0.35/kWh this peak window costs ~$9.89/evening.
[front_yard] The camera 'front_yard' is experiencing a loss of signal or frame reception error.

Despite the cameras showing the property as empty, the dogs are exhibiting persistent, urgent barking directed at the backyard and front door, indicating they are reacting strongly to an unseen stimulus. This sustained audio anomaly, coupled with multiple technical failures in the dog run and front yard cameras, suggests a potential external trigger or low-level activity that the visual sensors are missing.
Dogs appear to be home alone — no family member sighted in 5.8 hours (last seen ~12:04AM). Dogs are active in camera observations.

Despite the cameras showing the property as empty and quiet, the combination of urgent dog barking (04:47), multiple unclassified audio events, and persistent camera signal loss suggests an external stimulus—such as a passing animal or person—that is causing agitation but is either moving too quickly or is outside the camera's field of view.
[dog_run] The dog_run camera is not providing a live feed due to a technical error.

Despite the cameras showing the yard as empty, the persistent unclassified audio events and the high-severity flags regarding dog distress suggest an unknown, external stimulus is causing the dogs to react during the deep night. This pattern warrants checking for potential neighborhood disturbances or unusual activity that is not visible to the cameras.
[dog_run] The camera is experiencing a technical error, displaying 'No frames have been received, check error logs'.
